question: I want to build a cross-border payment or remittance business on Stellar; which stablecoin and SEP rails should I use, and how should I structure corridors with on- and off-ramps?

golden.answer:
Use `scout.searchResearch` to retrieve current, source-bounded results for this question. Report the source and as-of date. Treat returned rosters, ranks, counts, and current values as observations, not timeless facts.

Stable guardrails that remain in force: Frames Stellar as useful for fast low-cost settlement but not a complete remittance business by itself. Explains the corridor design needs source on-ramp, Stellar settlement asset, FX/quote path, destination off-ramp, KYC/AML/sanctions, liquidity, reconciliation, and customer support (the business architecture, not just a protocol). Drives stablecoin selection by issuer quality, liquidity, destination/geography, redemption, and regulatory fit (e.g. USDC vs EURC vs a local-currency stablecoin) rather than defaulting to XLM or one asset universally.
keyFacts: Retrieves source-bounded live results via scout.searchResearch and reports their source and as-of date without freezing a roster, rank, count, or current value.
Frames Stellar as useful for fast low-cost settlement but not a complete remittance business by itself.

Explains the corridor design needs source on-ramp, Stellar settlement asset, FX/quote path, destination off-ramp, KYC/AML/sanctions, liquidity, reconciliation, and customer support (the business architecture, not just a protocol).

Drives stablecoin selection by issuer quality, liquidity, destination/geography, redemption, and regulatory fit (e.g. USDC vs EURC vs a local-currency stablecoin) rather than defaulting to XLM or one asset universally.
avoid: Do NOT give legal or licensing advice as definitive for all jurisdictions.
Do NOT imply Stellar removes the need for fiat partners, KYC/AML, liquidity, or customer remediation.
Do NOT state a specific current TVL, count, roster, rank, or value as a timeless fact.
golden.notes:
2026-06-29: Differentiated toward stablecoin-selection + business architecture — promoted stablecoin-choice to a 5-weight must_have, demoted the SEP rail-map to should_have (the rail-map lane is owned by q-crp-become-an-anchor-licensing). Specific corridor/provider recommendations still require fresh provider checks; architecture/stablecoin-selection guidance is high confidence.
Also good if the answer: Maps relevant SEP rails as applicable: SEP-1 discovery, SEP-10 auth, SEP-12 KYC, SEP-24/6 deposit-withdrawal, SEP-31 cross-border payments, SEP-38 quotes. | Mentions integrating existing anchors is usually lower lift than becoming an anchor in every corridor.
Golden cites: Stellar SEP/anchor docs plus dated corridor/provider sources for concrete recommendations.

evidence: P4 Lane R behavioral conversion queue, row 16 (q-crp-remittance-founder-advisory): Behavioral golden: retrieve current source-bounded results, report an as-of/source, and do not pin a roster, rank, count, or current value.
Current source retained for behavioral retrieval: https://developers.stellar.org/docs/learn/fundamentals/anchors
